meaning: n.流沙,敏捷,危险而捉摸不定的事物
examples: The sandbank was uncertain, like quicksand under his feet.
On occasion, animals and plants have been preserved after becoming immersed in tar or quicksand, trapped in ice or lava flows, or engulfed by rapid falls of volcanic ash.
The research seemed founded on quicksand.
The road to women's equality, it turns out, is paved with potholes, quicksand and death traps.
It was like I was in quicksand up to my waist and I felt I wasn't gonna make it out alive.
